name: Alcaeus
shortDescription: Alcaeus is a minor Greek hero and son of Perseus. He is known primarily as an ancestor of Heracles.
description: Alcaeus is a figure in Greek mythology, primarily known as a hero and ancestor rather than a major deity. He is the son of Perseus and Andromeda, and therefore a descendant of Zeus. His significance stems mainly from his role in the lineage of Heracles, as he is considered the father of Amphitryon, who in turn was the mortal father of Heracles (Heracles' divine father was Zeus). While Alcaeus does not feature prominently in standalone myths or have dedicated cult worship, his connection to the legendary hero Heracles ensures his place within the broader mythological narrative. Some accounts suggest that Alcaeus was a strong and capable warrior, reflecting the heroic qualities attributed to his father, Perseus. His existence primarily serves to bridge the gap between the divine Perseus and the mortal lineage of Heracles, highlighting the intermingling of gods and mortals in Greek heroic tales. His story is often interwoven with that of his more famous descendants, underscoring the importance of genealogy and ancestry in establishing heroic credentials within Greek culture. He is not typically associated with specific domains or symbols beyond his general status as a hero and ancestor.
